title: Add Loop-on-Construct (cyclical sub-constructs)
description:
## Problem
Multi-node loops (review->revise cycle) need to be expressed as sub-constructs. This requires Loop modifier support on Construct, not just Node.

Construct already inherits Modifiable so pipe syntax works: construct | Loop(when=...). Need compiler, state, factory, and validation support.

## Fix (5 sub-parts)

### 5a. compiler.py — _add_subgraph handles Loop
In _add_subgraph (~line 115-142), after Oracle/Each checks, before plain subgraph:
- Check sub.has_modifier(Loop)
- Call new _add_subgraph_loop(graph, sub, sub_graph, subgraph_fn, loop, prev_node)
- _add_subgraph_loop: adds node, loop_router conditional edge, exit pass-through — same pattern as _add_loop_back_edge but for sub-constructs

### 5b. state.py — Loop on Construct gets append-list + counter
In compile_state_model sub_constructs section (~line 77-99):
- Add elif sub.has_modifier(Loop): append-list reducer + neo_loop_count field
```python
elif sub.has_modifier(Loop):
    fields[field_name] = (Annotated[list[sub.output], _append_loop_result], [])
    fields[f'neo_loop_count_{field_name}'] = (int, 0)
```

### 5c. factory.py — make_subgraph_fn: Loop counter + re-entry
- State update includes loop counter increment for Loop constructs
- On re-entry (iteration 2+), read input from own append-list field[-1] instead of scanning by type

### 5d. modifiers.py — Modifiable.__or__ dispatches validation
Currently calls validate_loop_self_edge which assumes Node (accesses .outputs/.inputs).
For Construct, dispatch to new validate_loop_construct that checks .output/.input:
```python
if isinstance(modifier, Loop):
    if hasattr(result, 'outputs'):  # Node
        validate_loop_self_edge(result)
    elif hasattr(result, 'output') and hasattr(result, 'input'):  # Construct
        validate_loop_construct(result)
```

### 5e. _construct_validation.py — validate_loop_construct
```python
def validate_loop_construct(construct) -> None:
    if construct.output is None or construct.input is None:
        raise ConstructError('Loop on Construct requires both input= and output=')
    if not _types_compatible(construct.output, construct.input):
        raise ConstructError(f'Loop on construct: output {construct.output} not compatible with input {construct.input}')
```

## Files
- src/neograph/compiler.py
- src/neograph/state.py
- src/neograph/factory.py
- src/neograph/modifiers.py
- src/neograph/_construct_validation.py

## Verification
- test_review_revise_cycle_exits_when_approved passes (rewritten to use sub-construct + Loop)
- New test: Loop on Construct with incompatible types raises ConstructError
- uv run pytest -q passes
design:
## Architecture
Same append-list reducer pattern as Node Loop. Three changes:
1. compiler._add_subgraph: check Loop modifier, wire back-edge with loop_router
2. state.compile_state_model: Loop Construct gets Annotated[list[T], _append_loop_result] + neo_loop_count
3. factory.make_subgraph_fn: increment counter in state update, read from own append-list on re-entry
notes:
## Findings
- Construct inherits Modifiable so pipe syntax works: construct | Loop(when=...)
- __or__ already dispatches to validate_loop_construct (added in neograph-1r6g commit 9fe0c12)
- compiler.py _add_subgraph handles Oracle/Each/Operator but not Loop — need to add Loop path
- state.py compile_state_model sub_constructs section needs Loop append-list + counter
- factory.py make_subgraph_fn needs: (a) loop counter increment, (b) re-entry from own append-list

## Relevant Code
- src/neograph/compiler.py:92-142 — _add_subgraph (add Loop handling after Each)
- src/neograph/state.py:77-99 — compile_state_model sub_constructs section
- src/neograph/factory.py:659-714 — make_subgraph_fn
- src/neograph/modifiers.py:81-89 — __or__ already dispatches validate_loop_construct
- src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:170-192 — validate_loop_construct (already exists)

## Core Invariant
Loop on Construct uses the same append-list reducer pattern as Loop on Node — one pattern for both.
close_reason: Loop-on-Construct implemented in 50a890a. All 10 loop tests pass.

title: Docs: NEOGRAPH_DEV=1 dev-mode warnings
description: Dev-mode warnings for ambiguous patterns (Oracle n=1, uneven models, Loop max_iterations=1, DAG summary). Undocumented. Needs a developer tools page.

title: Unused RunnableConfig as RC import in compiler.py
description: compiler.py:259 imports RunnableConfig as RC but RC is never used. Remove. Source: code review LOW-03, CON-09, PP-13.

title: Callable Node API for fan-in DAGs: node2(node1(...))
description:
Source: Keras Functional API, Pulumi Output[T]
Effort: Medium
Breaks API: No (additive, but this is what Construct should have been)

Current nodes=[a, b, c] is implicitly linear. True DAGs with fan-in require sub-Constructs today. Proposed: Node.__call__ returns a typed NodeHandle[OutputType]; chaining handles builds the graph.

  claims   = decompose()                # NodeHandle[Claims]
  scored   = enrich(claims)             # NodeHandle[ScoredClaims]
  verified = verify(claims, scored)     # fan-in, free
  pipeline = Construct('rw', output=verified)

Wins:
- True DAG with fan-in that current nodes=[] list can't express
- Reads like data flow
- Type-safe: mypy checks claims is Claims, scored is ScoredClaims

Implementation:
- NodeHandle[T] class wrapping (node, output_type)
- Node.__call__ returns NodeHandle
- Construct accepts an output= NodeHandle and walks backward to collect all nodes
- Coexists with current nodes=[...] list mode

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #5
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by @node + ForwardConstruct. The callable-node API (node2(node1(...))) is exactly what ForwardConstruct.forward() provides (self.classify(self.decompose(topic))). And @node's parameter-name inference (def classify(decompose: Claims)) auto-wires the same topology without explicit call syntax. Both primary APIs make this feature redundant.

Implement beads task neograph-dqe -- make the failing test pass (TDD green),
then refactor.

## Workflow

1. **Green**: Write minimum code to make the failing test pass
2. **Verify**: Run the SAME test that FAILED -- must now PASS
3. **Refactor**: With passing tests as safety net
4. **Quality gate**: Run project quality command

## When EXISTING Tests Fail

1. STOP. Do NOT modify the failing tests.
2. Re-read the Core Invariant.
3. If your approach violates the invariant -> revert and rethink.
4. If the test has a genuine bug -> document WHY before modifying.

Sacred rule: NEVER adjust tests to match code without justification.

## If Successful

Close the original beads task:
```bash
bd close neograph-dqe
```
acceptance_criteria: Quality command passes. Regression test passes. Original task closed.

title: Produce mode: support json_mode and text extraction strategies alongside with_structured_output
description: Produce mode always uses llm.with_structured_output(model) which fails on DeepSeek Reasoner/V3 (400: response_format type unavailable). Production pattern: inject JSON schema into prompt + bind response_format=json_object + parse manually. Add llm_config.output_strategy: structured (current), json_mode (schema-in-prompt, widest compat), text (plain text + parse). Default should be json_mode for widest provider support.

title: Typed llm_config via Literal-discriminated overloads (Node.produce, Node.gather)
description:
Source: Rust typestate builder
Effort: Small-medium
Breaks API: Mild (additive, deprecate Node(mode=...) eventually)

Problem: Node(mode='produce', output=Claims, tools=[...]) accepts invalid combinations silently at runtime. mypy has no idea that produce mode doesn't support tools.

Proposed:
  decompose = Node.produce('decompose', output=Claims, model='fast', prompt='x', temperature=0.8)
  verify    = Node.gather('verify', input=Cluster, output=VerifyResult, tools=[search])
  execute   = Node.execute('execute', input=Plan, output=Result, tools=[write_file])
  munge     = Node.scripted('munge', fn='munge_fn', output=Data)

Each factory uses @overload so Node.produce(tools=[...]) is a type error.

Wins:
- Eliminates runtime 'can't use tools in produce mode' surprises
- Autocomplete shows only valid parameters per mode
- mypy/pyright catch misconfigurations statically

Implementation: add Node.produce, Node.gather, Node.execute classmethods with @overload decorators. Keep Node(mode=...) for backward compat with a deprecation warning.

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #7
close_reason: LOW VALUE now that @node is the primary API. Typed llm_config via Literal overloads was about catching Node(mode='produce') without output at mypy level. @node's mode inference + decoration-time validation (neograph-q54) already catches these errors eagerly with clear messages. The Node IR level benefit is marginal — advanced users who construct Node() directly are expected to read docs.

title: BDD naming: 4 test files at 0% adoption
description: test_node_decorator.py (12%), test_modifiers.py (3%), test_composition.py (0%), test_forward.py (0%), test_pipeline_modes.py (0%) still use old naming. Apply test_{what_should_happen}_when_{condition} pattern to all test methods. Mechanical rename, no logic changes.
close_reason: Applied BDD naming convention to all test methods in 5 files: test_node_decorator.py (78 tests), test_modifiers.py (34 tests), test_composition.py (29 tests), test_forward.py (36 tests), test_pipeline_modes.py (40 tests). All 333 tests pass.

title: LOW-13: Global _scripted_registry pollution without cleanup in tests
description: Tests that call register_scripted never clean up. Registry grows monotonically. Names are unique so no collisions today, but doesn't scale. Consider snapshot/restore fixture.
close_reason: Already handled: tests/conftest.py has autouse _clean_registries fixture that clears _scripted_registry before every test

title: Operator condition error masked by checkpointer guard
description:
Operator(when='nonexistent') without a checkpointer gives 'no checkpointer' error instead of 'condition not registered'. The checkpointer guard fires first, hiding the real problem.

Root cause: compiler.py compile() checks has_operator+checkpointer before reaching lookup_condition in _add_operator_check. Error ordering is confusing but the error does fire.

Fix: Move the condition existence check before the checkpointer guard, or add a separate validation pass for condition strings at the top of compile().

Fixture: tests/check_fixtures/known_gaps/mod_operator_unregistered_condition.py
close_reason: Fixed: condition check fires before checkpointer guard

title: HIGH-02: Extract shared _build_annotation_namespace helper
description: New (kqd refactor). Frame-walking namespace builder duplicated ~25 lines between _classify_di_params (decorators.py:194-225) and @node inputs inference (decorators.py:521-552). Extract _build_annotation_namespace(f, frame_depth) -> dict shared helper.
close_reason: Extracted _build_annotation_namespace helper; both _classify_di_params and node() input inference use it

title: FromInput[PydanticModel] — bundle multiple input fields into a typed context object
description: Scripted @nodes that need pipeline metadata (node_id, project_root, run config fields) currently repeat: node_id: FromInput[str], project_root: FromInput[str], shared: FromConfig[SharedResources] — 3 lines per node, ~20 nodes in piarch = 60 lines of boilerplate. Proposal: support FromInput[PydanticModel] where the model's field names map to config['configurable'] keys. Example: class RunCtx(BaseModel): node_id: str; project_root: str. Then: def my_node(upstream: X, ctx: FromInput[RunCtx]) -> Y: ... and ctx.node_id / ctx.project_root are populated from config automatically. Each field resolved independently via config[configurable][field_name].
close_reason: 6jd: FromInput[PydanticModel] and FromConfig[PydanticModel] now detected at decoration time and resolved at runtime by pulling each of the model's field names from config['configurable'], constructing the instance. Classifier walks call-stack frames to find locally-defined Pydantic model classes (needed under 'from __future__ import annotations'). Shared _classify_di_params + _resolve_di_value helpers extracted so both @node and @merge_fn use the same DI machinery. 4 regression tests in TestFromInputPydanticModel. 9zj: new @merge_fn decorator for Oracle merge functions with FromInput/FromConfig DI. Auto-registers via register_scripted for back-compat with Oracle(merge_fn='name') lookups. make_oracle_merge_fn in factory.py checks the @merge_fn registry first and calls with resolved DI args instead of legacy (variants, config) signature when a match is found. Plain (variants, config) merge_fn functions still work unchanged. 3 tests in TestOracleMergeFnDI covering bundled FromConfig, FromInput, and back-compat. 217/217 passing.

title: Fix: stale ValueError docstring in TestToolRegistrationError
description: test_pipeline_modes.py::TestToolRegistrationError::test_clear_error_raised_when_tool_not_registered — docstring says 'raises ValueError' but code correctly expects ConfigurationError. Update the docstring.

title: Tighten test_map_end_to_end_fanout assertion (and TestEach:319)
description:
HIGH-01 from code review 040426_2207.

The only end-to-end fan-out test uses:
  assert "alpha" in verify_results or len(verify_results) == 2
The 'or' short-circuits — a single-item dict {'alpha': ...} passes even though fan-out should produce two keys. A regression that broke beta dispatch would not be caught.

Verification: python -c "d={'alpha':'x'}; print('alpha' in d or len(d)==2)" → True.

Fix tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py:386 and the copy-pasted source at tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py:319 (TestEach::test_fanout_over_collection) with:
  assert set(verify_results.keys()) == {'alpha', 'beta'}
  assert verify_results['alpha'].cluster_label == 'alpha'
  assert verify_results['beta'].cluster_label == 'beta'

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md HIGH-01
close_reason: Wave 1: HIGH-01 tightened fan-out assertion in TestEach::test_fanout_over_collection (line 319) and TestNodeMap::test_map_end_to_end_fanout (line 386) with set equality + per-key payload. HIGH-02 renamed construct.py's _type_name to _fmt_type (7 call sites); factory.py's _type_name stays. 99/99 passing.

title: Research neograph-drbd
description:
Execute research for beads task neograph-drbd. All findings go into the
bead itself -- NOT the filesystem.

## Instructions

1. **Read the task**: `bd show neograph-drbd`
2. **Spec check** (if task touches schemas/protocol):
   - Read relevant specs and schemas
   - Check required vs optional, type constraints
3. **Explore the codebase**:
   - Search for relevant code
   - Read files that will need modification
   - Check existing tests for the affected area
   - Find 2-3 similar implementations to follow as patterns
4. **Check documentation** (doc-first rule)
5. **Engineering checklist** -- answer concretely:
   - DRY: Does similar logic exist?
   - Library idioms: How do your frameworks solve this?
   - Data flow trace: Walk one concrete example through the full chain
   - Existing conventions: What pattern do similar implementations follow?
   - Test infrastructure: What fixtures/helpers exist?
6. **Identify architecture decisions**
7. **Extract the core invariant** -- State in ONE sentence the architectural
   principle that ALL changes for this task must preserve.

## Store Research in the Bead

```bash
bd update neograph-drbd --append-notes "## Findings
- [key findings]

## Relevant Code
- path/to/file.py:line -- [description]

## Risks & Edge Cases
- [potential issues]"

bd update neograph-drbd --design "## Core Invariant
[One sentence: the architectural principle every change must preserve]

## Implementation Plan
1. [first step]
2. [second step]"
```

## Gate: Add Label

ONLY after notes AND design are written:
```bash
bd label add neograph-drbd research:complete
```
acceptance_criteria: Task has research:complete label. Notes contain Findings. Design contains Core Invariant + Implementation Plan.
close_reason: Research complete: task description has full spec

title: Architect review of neograph-drbd

Run `bd show neograph-drbd` and verify research:complete label is present.

## Review Criteria

1. **Invariant alignment**: Does EVERY step maintain the Core Invariant?
2. **Approach soundness**: Will the plan work?
3. **Risk coverage**: Are risks identified and covered?
4. **Pattern compliance**: Does the plan follow project architecture patterns?
5. **Specificity**: Is the plan specific enough to implement without guessing?
6. **Alternatives**: Was a better approach missed?

## Rate Each Finding

- **LOW**: Minor suggestion, cosmetic, nice-to-have
- **MEDIUM**: Real concern but agent can resolve autonomously
- **HIGH**: Fundamental issue -- user input required

```bash
bd update neograph-drbd --append-notes "## Architect Review

Overall: [ALL_LOW | NEEDS_REFINEMENT | NEEDS_USER_INPUT]"
```

title: @node: Operator interrupt_when kwarg for human-in-loop
description:
Part of epic neograph-4mx (@node production readiness). Surfaced by the Path A Dogfood epic.

## Problem

The Operator modifier (`src/neograph/modifiers.py:175-188`) provides human-in-loop via `node | Operator(when='condition_name')` where `when` is a registered condition function. There is no way to attach an Operator modifier to a @node-decorated function, so pipelines with interrupt/resume semantics cannot use @node.

Also: the existing `when='string_key'` pattern is itself a target of the neograph-pub epic (forward() mode kills string dispatch). Adding Operator support to @node is a bridge until forward() lands, but it still has to look clean to make @node the sole documented API.

## Proposed API

Two forms, both via a new `interrupt_when=` kwarg on `@node`:

### Form 1: registered condition name (mirrors existing Operator(when=...))
    @node(mode='scripted', output=ValidationResult, interrupt_when='validation_failed')
    def validate(claims: Claims) -> ValidationResult: ...

Internally attaches `Operator(when='validation_failed')` to the Node.

### Form 2: inline callable (the cleaner form — kills string dispatch)
    @node(mode='scripted', output=ValidationResult,
          interrupt_when=lambda state: (
              {'issues': state.validate.issues}
              if state.validate and not state.validate.passed
              else None
          ))
    def validate(claims: Claims) -> ValidationResult: ...

The callable is registered under a synthesized name (`_node_interrupt_{node_name}_{id}`) via `register_condition`, then attached as `Operator(when=<synthesized_name>)`. The user never sees the synthesized name.

## Implementation sketch

In `src/neograph/decorators.py`:

    from neograph.modifiers import Operator

    def node(
        fn=None,
        *,
        ...,
        interrupt_when: str | Callable | None = None,
    ):
        def decorator(f):
            ...
            n = Node(...)
            _register_sidecar(n, f, param_names)

            if interrupt_when is not None:
                from neograph.factory import register_condition

                if isinstance(interrupt_when, str):
                    condition_name = interrupt_when
                elif callable(interrupt_when):
                    condition_name = f\"_node_interrupt_{node_name}_{id(f):x}\"
                    register_condition(condition_name, interrupt_when)
                else:
                    raise ConstructError(
                        f\"@node '{node_name}' interrupt_when must be a string \"
                        f\"(registered condition name) or a callable; got \"
                        f\"{type(interrupt_when).__name__}\"
                    )

                n = n | Operator(when=condition_name)
                _register_sidecar(n, f, param_names)  # sidecar carryover

            return n

## Acceptance criteria

- New tests in `TestNodeDecorator`:
  - `test_node_decorator_interrupt_with_string_name`: uses a pre-registered condition name, verifies the Operator modifier is attached and the interrupt fires end-to-end with a MemorySaver checkpointer (mirrors `TestOperator::test_interrupt_on_failure`).
  - `test_node_decorator_interrupt_with_callable`: uses an inline lambda condition, verifies auto-registration under a synthesized name and end-to-end interrupt behavior.
  - `test_node_decorator_interrupt_resume`: verifies interrupt + resume flow (mirrors `TestOperatorResume::test_interrupt_then_resume`).
  - `test_node_decorator_interrupt_when_falsy_continues`: condition returns None, graph runs through without pausing (mirrors `TestOperatorContinues`).
  - `test_node_decorator_interrupt_when_wrong_type_raises`: passing e.g. an int raises ConstructError at decoration time.
- Operator modifier attached to Node's `modifiers` list — verifiable via `node.get_modifier(Operator)`.
- Existing TestOperator tests still pass.
- @node docstring updated with an interrupt example.
- Full suite stays green.

## Dependencies

- Blocks on **neograph-jyw** (sidecar carryover on modifier composition).

## Files to read

- `src/neograph/modifiers.py:175-188` — Operator class
- `src/neograph/factory.py::register_condition`, `lookup_condition` — condition registry
- `src/neograph/compiler.py::_add_operator_check` — how Operator compiles to interrupt
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestOperator`, `TestOperatorContinues`, `TestOperatorResume` — patterns to mirror

## Non-goals

- Do NOT replace the Operator modifier class itself — @node is a sugar layer over it.
- Do NOT add support for multiple interrupt_when kwargs on a single @node — one is enough for v1.
- Do NOT pre-empt forward() mode's control-flow handling — that's neograph-pub epic scope.
close_reason: Three modifier-kwargs subtasks on neograph-4mx. nqe: Oracle ensemble_n/merge_fn/merge_prompt kwargs, 6 tests. t5z: mode='raw' support replacing @raw_node, 4 tests. 6y3: Operator interrupt_when kwarg (string + callable forms with auto-registration), 6 tests. 143/143 passing (+16 new).

title: json_mode output schema: emit TypeScript-style notation instead of JSON Schema
description:
Sibling of neograph-ni6 (input rendering). This issue covers OUTPUT schema emission.

## The problem

Current json_mode output strategy relies on consumers to inject a JSON Schema into the prompt so the LLM knows what shape to return. piarch's bridge does this via:

    schema_str = json.dumps(output_model.model_json_schema(), indent=2)
    # Injected into prompt via 'common/json-instruction' template

JSON Schema is verbose. For a typical Pydantic model with 5-10 fields and some nesting, the JSON Schema representation is 300-800 tokens. BAML's insight: a TypeScript-style notation for the same schema is ~4x more token-efficient AND the LLM parses it more reliably.

## The BAML comparison

Same Resume model, two representations:

JSON Schema (current, ~400 tokens):
{
  '$schema': 'https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema',
  'type': 'object',
  'properties': {
    'name': {'type': 'string', 'title': 'Name'},
    'education': {
      'type': 'array',
      'items': {
        'type': 'object',
        'properties': {
          'school': {'type': 'string'},
          'graduation_year': {'type': 'integer'}
        },
        'required': ['school', 'graduation_year']
      }
    }
  },
  'required': ['name', 'education']
}

TypeScript-style (proposed, ~80 tokens):
{
  name: string
  education: [
    {
      school: string
      graduation_year: int
    }
  ]
}

5x reduction for the same information. BAML's docs and benchmarks claim the LLM parses the TypeScript form more reliably too — fewer parse failures, fewer schema drift errors.

## Proposed design

Add a new helper function to neograph:

    from neograph import describe_type

    describe_type(
        model: type[BaseModel],
        *,
        prefix: str = 'Answer in JSON matching this schema:',
        hoist_classes: Literal['auto', 'all'] | list[str] = 'auto',
        hoisted_class_prefix: str = 'type ',
        always_hoist_enums: bool = True,
        or_splitter: str = ' or ',  # NOT '|' — LLMs parse 'or' better
        indent: str = '  ',
    ) -> str

Behavior:
- Walks Pydantic model_fields recursively
- Emits TypeScript-style notation (string/int/float/bool primitives, [ ] for lists, { } for objects, enum members inlined)
- 'hoist_classes=auto' extracts repeated nested types as 'type Foo = { ... }' declarations before the main type — avoids repetition when the same sub-model appears multiple times
- 'or_splitter=" or "' for Union types (BAML's insight: LLMs read 'string or int' better than 'string | int')
- Reads Pydantic Field(description='...') metadata and emits as inline comments: '  name: string  // the person name'
- Handles Literal types as inlined enums: 'status: "active" or "inactive" or "pending"'

## Integration with output_strategy

Change json_mode strategy in neograph/_llm.py to use describe_type() instead of raw JSON Schema:

    # Before:
    schema_str = json.dumps(output_model.model_json_schema(), indent=2)

    # After:
    schema_str = describe_type(output_model)

This is a strategy-level change in neograph, not a consumer change. Consumers using json_mode get the improvement automatically.

Leave structured strategy unchanged (LangChain's with_structured_output uses the provider's native JSON Schema and that's fine — providers optimize for it).

Leave text strategy unchanged.

## Public helper

Expose describe_type() as a public helper (neograph.describe_type) so consumers who write custom prompts can use it directly. Example piarch use:

    @node(output=BRDocument, model='reason', prompt='br/write-document')
    def br_write(qualification: BRQualification) -> BRDocument: ...

    # In the prompt template:
    'Return a business rule matching this schema:\n{output_schema}\n\nBased on: {qualification}'

    # prompt_compiler:
    messages[0].content = messages[0].content.format(
        output_schema=describe_type(BRDocument),
        qualification=rendered_qualification,
    )

## IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: port from BAML

BAML's implementation is in Rust (baml/engine/baml-lib/jinja-runtime/src/output_format/types.rs) and is the best prior art. Port the algorithm to Python:

- Core type dispatch (primitive/list/object/enum/union)
- hoist_classes logic (auto-detect repeated types, emit as 'type Foo = ...' declarations)
- Field description inlining from Pydantic Field metadata
- or_splitter for unions
- always_hoist_enums behavior

Do NOT take BAML as a dependency — it's Rust-first with a Python client, designed for .baml file consumption. Use only as the reference implementation for the algorithm.

## Token savings estimate

For piarch's 60-80 models across 15 pipelines:

- Average JSON Schema per model: 400 tokens
- Average TypeScript notation per model: 80 tokens
- Average produce calls per pipeline run: 5
- Average pipeline runs per day during active iteration: 50

Savings: (400 - 80) × 5 × 50 = 80,000 tokens/day, or ~2.4M tokens/month.
At ~$0.5/1M input tokens (DeepSeek R1 rate), that's ~$1.20/month in savings. Small, but compounds with scale.

The real value isn't cost — it's prompt parse reliability. Fewer schema drift errors = fewer fallback-path executions = more deterministic outputs.

## Success criteria

1. describe_type(BRDocument) produces <100 tokens for models that currently emit 400+ tokens of JSON Schema
2. json_mode strategy automatically uses describe_type() instead of raw model_json_schema()
3. Public helper available as 'from neograph import describe_type'
4. Nested models with repetition get hoisted correctly ('auto' mode)
5. Pydantic Field(description=...) metadata is surfaced as inline comments
6. Union types use 'or' separator, not '|'
7. Enum Literal types are inlined as quoted strings
8. Round-trip test: LLM response parsed by Pydantic matches expected structure for complex models
design:
## Architecture (from plan session 2026-04-06)

### New file: src/neograph/describe_type.py
Public helper: `from neograph import describe_type`

```python
def describe_type(
    model: type[BaseModel],
    *,
    prefix: str = 'Answer in JSON matching this schema:',
    hoist_classes: Literal['auto', 'all'] | list[str] = 'auto',
    always_hoist_enums: bool = True,
    or_splitter: str = ' or ',
    indent: str = '  ',
) -> str
```

### Algorithm: two-pass recursive type walker
- Pass 1: _count_classes(model) — walk recursively, count occurrences in a dict
- Pass 2: _render_model_body(model, ...) — render, using references for hoisted classes

Type dispatch: str->string, int->int, float->float, bool->bool, None->null, list[X]->[X], dict[K,V]->object notation, Optional[X]->X or null, Union[A,B]->joined by or_splitter, Literal['a','b']->quoted values with or, BaseModel->recursive { field: type } blocks, Field(description=...)->// inline comment.

Handle circular models via visited set.

### Integration: json_mode auto-injects output_schema
- _llm.py:invoke_structured generates output_schema=describe_type(output_model) for json_mode
- _compile_prompt passes output_schema as new kwarg
- _accepted_params mechanism ensures old prompt_compilers that don't accept output_schema= just don't receive it (backward compat)

### Files to modify
- src/neograph/describe_type.py (NEW)
- src/neograph/_llm.py (_compile_prompt + invoke_structured)
- src/neograph/__init__.py (export)

### Tests (21 tests in 2 classes)
TestDescribeType (17): primitives, nesting, lists, dicts, optionals, unions, literals, enums, field descriptions, hoisting auto/all/explicit, recursion, empty model, token reduction comparison
TestJsonModeOutputSchema (4): json_mode passes output_schema, structured/text don't, backward compat

### Core invariant
describe_type is a pure function with no side effects. It walks Pydantic model_fields only. The json_mode integration is additive — existing prompt_compilers continue to work unchanged.
notes:
## Research note: BAML as a library dep is not viable

Investigated whether baml-py (Rust+PyO3, like pydantic-core) exposes the output_format renderer as a standalone Python function. Verdict: NO.

**Findings** (verified from baml-py canary branch):

- baml-py public API is entirely BAML runtime: BamlRuntime, FunctionResult, streams, media types, observability. No standalone schema renderer.
- TypeBuilder helper exists for dynamic type construction but has no .render() method returning schema string.
- Rust crate baml-lib/jsonish holds the algorithm but is not exposed via PyO3 bindings.
- BAML tracks this gap in issues #998 and #1403, shipped 'Modular API' (b.request/b.parse) but still requires .baml files + codegen.

**Why PyO3 being available doesn't help**:

pydantic-core is DESIGNED as a library primitive with a stable documented API. baml-py is DESIGNED as a runtime consumer of .baml files. Same tech (PyO3), fundamentally different API contracts. BAML chose to only expose the runtime, not the primitives.

**Implementation still means porting**:

- Port from baml-lib/jsonish Rust source (open, well-documented)
- ~200-400 lines of Python
- Algorithm is simple (type dispatch + recursion + hoisting)
- Performance is not critical (rendered <1KB per LLM call, not a hot loop)

**Optional upstream contribution**:

File an issue with BoundaryML asking to expose OutputFormatContent renderer as a standalone Python function. If shipped, our port becomes replaceable. Good open-source citizen move, benefits every other Python consumer.
close_reason: describe_type() landed + json_mode auto-injects output_schema via _compile_prompt.

title: Bug: dict-form outputs + Oracle causes concurrent write error
description: Oracle's make_oracle_redirect_fn doesn't handle dict-form per-key state fields. When a node has outputs={'result': X, 'meta': Y} | Oracle(n=2, merge_fn=...), the Oracle redirect creates N variant subgraphs that each try to write per-key fields, causing concurrent write errors in LangGraph's state management. Discovered by executor-modifiers during rdu.6 test writing — the test was silently downgraded to single-type outputs. Need to fix make_oracle_redirect_fn to handle dict-form outputs, then write the real integration test.
design:
## Root Cause
make_oracle_redirect_fn (factory.py:437) assumes single-output: it checks result.get(field_name). With dict-form outputs, _build_state_update writes keys like dogen_result, dogen_meta — never dogen. So val is None, redirect returns raw result, both generators write to LastValue channels that only accept 1 value.

## Fix
make_oracle_redirect_fn needs to detect dict-form output keys (field_name_*) and redirect ALL of them to the collector. The merge_fn needs to merge per-key and write back to all per-key fields. Alternatively, collect the full result dict into the collector and let the merge_fn handle the dict shape.
notes:
## Reproduction
- Failing test: tests/test_modifiers.py::TestModifierCombinations::test_oracle_merges_per_key_when_dict_form_outputs
- Root cause: make_oracle_redirect_fn looks for result.get(field_name) but dict-form outputs write to {field_name}_{key}. Redirect misses them → generators write directly to per-key LastValue channels → InvalidUpdateError from LangGraph.
## Trace-similar
- make_each_redirect_fn (line 563) has the same result.get(field_name) pattern
- But Each + dict-form works by accident: _build_state_update already wraps per-key with {key_val: val}, so the redirect is a no-op and the raw result passes through correctly
- Oracle is different: the redirect MUST move results to the collector (list reducer), not the consumer-facing fields. With dict-form, the redirect misses per-key fields and they go directly to LastValue channels
- No other similar patterns found outside factory.py
close_reason: Fixed: redirect fn handles per-key fields, state model uses single collector for dict-form+Oracle, merge fn unwraps per-key

title: Factory: loop counter increment in state update
description:
Node wrappers increment neo_loop_count on each execution when Loop modifier present.

## File: src/neograph/factory.py

In _build_state_update (or a new helper), when node has Loop modifier:

    loop_mod = node.get_modifier(Loop)
    if loop_mod:
        count_field = f'neo_loop_count_{field_name}'
        current = _state_get(state, count_field) or 0
        update[count_field] = current + 1
        if loop_mod.history:
            history_field = f'neo_loop_history_{field_name}'
            update[history_field] = result  # appended via reducer

This must work for scripted, produce, and agent/act wrappers.

## Acceptance criteria
- neo_loop_count increments on each loop iteration
- history=True collects each iteration's result
- Counter resets correctly for Each+Loop (per-item independent)
close_reason: Added loop counter increment to _build_state_update in factory.py. When a node has Loop modifier: increments neo_loop_count_{field_name} on each execution, and if loop_mod.history is True, writes result to neo_loop_history_{field_name}. Works for all node types (scripted, produce, agent/act) since all call _build_state_update. All 420 existing tests pass.

title: required=True broken for bundled BaseModel FromInput/FromConfig
description:
_resolve_di_value extracts the required flag for bundled models but never enforces it. When model construction fails (missing fields), it catches the exception and returns None with a warning — even when required=True. Inconsistent with the scalar case which raises ExecutionError.

Root cause: decorators.py lines 340-357 — from_input_model/from_config_model branch extracts _required but does not check it.

Fix: Before attempting model construction, iterate model_fields and collect missing ones. If required=True and any fields are missing, raise ExecutionError listing the missing fields.

Fixtures: tests/check_fixtures/known_gaps/di_required_bundled_model.py, di_required_missing_no_config.py
close_reason: Fixed: required=True now enforced for bundled BaseModel DI params at runtime

title: Dogfood: migrate full/mixed pipelines (01 consolidation, 10, observable_pipeline) to @node
description:
Part of epic neograph-eiw (Dogfood @node). Blocks on multiple neograph-4mx subtasks depending on the specific patterns each example uses.

## Scope

Migrate the biggest examples — they combine many modifiers and modes, so they're the most useful stress tests for @node's completeness:

1. **Consolidate `01_scripted_pipeline.py` and `01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py`** — right now we have both; pick one canonical version. Options: delete 01 (declarative version) and rename 01b to 01. Or keep both with 01 as "here's the low-level IR" and 01b as "here's the @node way". Decision: delete 01, rename 01b to 01, and the @node path becomes THE scripted-pipeline example.

2. **`examples/10_full_pipeline.py`** — full verification pipeline with discover + verify + report. Uses Each (fan-out over clusters), mixed scripted + produce modes. This is the marquee example for @node dogfooding.

3. **`examples/observable_pipeline.py`** — observability showcase. Verify @node preserves every log/metric/tracing field and the pipeline stays observable after migration.

## What to do

1. Wait for blockers: neograph-jyw (Each), neograph-8pt (fan-in validation), neograph-q54 (mode inference), neograph-nqe (Oracle if used), neograph-6y3 (Operator if used), neograph-t5z (raw if used).
2. Read each example to understand what modifiers/modes it uses.
3. Migrate incrementally: one example at a time. After each, run it end-to-end and diff output against pre-migration.
4. For 01 consolidation: delete `01_scripted_pipeline.py`, rename `01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py` to `01_scripted_pipeline.py`, update its top docstring.
5. For each file, update the top-of-file docstring to explain what patterns it demonstrates and why @node is the right choice.

## Acceptance criteria

- `01_scripted_pipeline.py` exists as the @node version; the `01b_*` file is removed.
- `10_full_pipeline.py` uses @node for all node declarations and construct_from_module for assembly.
- `observable_pipeline.py` uses @node; all observability fields still populated (verify via log inspection or explicit assertions).
- Each example runs end-to-end with output equivalent to pre-migration.
- Full test suite stays green.
- Any @node gap surfaced → file a subtask on neograph-4mx.

## Dependencies

- **neograph-jyw** — Each interop (for 04, 10)
- **neograph-8pt** — fan-in validation (for 10's fan-in nodes)
- **neograph-q54** — mode inference (cleaner example code)
- **Others as needed** — depending on what 10 and observable_pipeline actually use

## Files to read

- `examples/01_scripted_pipeline.py`
- `examples/01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py`
- `examples/10_full_pipeline.py`
- `examples/observable_pipeline.py`

## Non-goals

- Do NOT touch examples 02-09, 11 — those are other subtasks.
- Do NOT add new functionality; exact-behavior migration only.
close_reason: Final dogfood wave. gws: example 09 + TestOperator/TestOperatorContinues/TestOperatorResume migrated to @node(interrupt_when=). 7qn: 01/01b consolidated (@node version is now 01, declarative deleted), example 10 partially migrated (decompose→@node, rest stays declarative due to sub-construct), observable_pipeline fully migrated. 1kz: __init__.py docstring rewritten @node-first, __all__ reordered with section comments, README.md created (74 lines, @node quickstart), examples/README.md created (14 examples indexed). 159/159 passing.

title: TestLLMUnknownToolCall assertion is vacuous
description: test_unknown_tool_call_handled asserts result['explore'] is not None but the value is an AIMessage('ok done'), not a Claims model. Test passes because AIMessage is not None, but production output is wrong. Fix: assert isinstance(result['explore'], Claims) and fix FakeLLMHallucinator to return proper structured output. title: Quick Start topic passed through input is silently unused
description:
website/src/content/docs/getting-started/quick-start.mdx:120,157 passes 'topic' in run(input=...), but line 157 hardcodes 'microservice authentication' in the prompt compiler. A learner reading this will assume topic is automatically threaded into the prompt — it isn't. They'll later be surprised that changing the topic value changes nothing.

Fix: show the correct pattern — read topic from config["configurable"]["topic"] in the prompt compiler (as examples/vs_langgraph/01_sequential_pipeline.py:99 does). This teaches the config injection pattern explicitly. Source: code review issue #6.

title: Fix: TestThreeSurfaceFanInParity declarative/programmatic builders are identical
description: The parametrized fan-in parity tests in test_validation.py claim 3-surface coverage but _fan_in_valid_declarative and _fan_in_valid_programmatic are functionally identical (both use _producer + Node.scripted). Only 2 distinct surfaces are actually tested. Fix: make the declarative builder use .map() style or Node with single-type inputs (the actual declarative surface), so the three parametrized variants exercise genuinely different code paths.
notes:
## Research
- _fan_in_valid_declarative (line 802) and _fan_in_valid_programmatic (line 834) are copy-paste identical
- Same for _fan_in_mismatch_declarative (846) and _fan_in_mismatch_programmatic (878)
- Fix: declarative builder should use single-type inputs=Type (the classic Construct/Node.scripted form) instead of dict-form inputs={...}
- The declarative surface's distinctive feature is single-type inputs where the validator does isinstance scanning, not dict-form keyed lookup
close_reason: Declarative builders now use single-type inputs (isinstance scan path), genuinely different from programmatic dict-form builders

title: Validator: back-edge type compatibility for Loop
description:
Assembly-time validation that Loop back-edge is type-safe.

## File: src/neograph/_construct_validation.py

In _validate_node_chain, after existing modifier checks:

For nodes with Loop modifier:
- Self-loop (reenter=None): node.outputs must be compatible with node.inputs via _types_compatible. If dict-form inputs, the key matching the node's own name must be compatible.
- Multi-node (reenter=X): node.outputs must be compatible with the reentry node's inputs. Look up the reentry node by name in the construct's node list.

Error message:
    ConstructError: Loop on node 'revise' back-edge to 'review': output type Draft
    not compatible with reentry input type ReviewInput.
    hint: the loop's last node output must match the reentry node's input type.

## Acceptance criteria
- Self-loop type mismatch raises ConstructError
- Multi-node reenter type mismatch raises ConstructError
- Compatible types pass silently
- test_loop.py::TestLoopValidation::test_raises_when_loop_output_incompatible_with_input passes
close_reason: Added Loop back-edge type validation: validate_loop_self_edge() for self-loops at | time, _check_loop_reenter() for multi-node reenter at construct assembly time. Modified _construct_validation.py (main logic) and modifiers.py (wire-up in __or__). All 420 existing tests pass.

title: Docs: context= field walkthrough
description: context= injects verbatim state fields into LLM prompts. Listed in API reference and spec format but no walkthrough. Example 14 exists as reference.

title: Each modifier: downstream consumers of fan-out don't know the aggregated dict type
description:
## Original report
After Node(output=X).map(...), the Each wraps results as dict[key, X]. Downstream consumer with input=dict fails validator (dict type bypass is broken, see neograph-ebe). Using input=X also wrong because the actual state field is a dict. Solution: validator should track Each output as dict[str, X] so downstream input=dict matches. Alternative: provide an explicit EachResult[X] type or Node.each_output attribute for the aggregated shape.

## Triage (2026-04-04)

**Reproduction confirmed**: A chain `[Node(output=Clusters), Node(input=ClusterGroup, output=MatchResult).map(...), Node(input=MatchResult, output=Summary)]` passes the validator as "legal", but at runtime the third node's `_extract_input` calls `isinstance(state.verify, MatchResult)` on a `dict[str, MatchResult]` and gets False, returning None silently.

This is a **false-positive validation + silent runtime failure** — the exact class of bug the assembly-time validator exists to prevent.

**Root cause** (`_construct_validation.py:50-65`):
```python
for item in construct.nodes:
    ...
    output_type = getattr(item, "output", None)
    ...
    producers.append((field_name, output_type, label))
```
The producer's stored `output_type` is the node's raw declared `output`, ignoring modifiers that change the effective state shape.

**State builder ground truth** (`state.py:119-124`, for a Node with Each; `state.py:75-81` for a Construct with Each):
- Each modifier → state field is `dict[str, node.output] | None` with a `_merge_dicts` reducer
- Oracle modifier → state field stays `node.output | None` (no shape change)
- No modifier → state field is `node.output | None`

The validator must track the modifier-adjusted effective type to match what the state builder will emit.

## Proposed fix (depends on neograph-ebe being merged first)

**Part 1**: in `_validate_node_chain`, when appending a producer from an item with an `Each` modifier, wrap the output type:
```python
effective_output = output_type
get_mod = getattr(item, "get_modifier", None)
if get_mod and get_mod(Each) is not None:
    # Each aggregates results as a dict keyed by Each.key. The state
    # builder emits this as dict[str, output_type] — see state.py:119-124.
    effective_output = dict[str, output_type]
producers.append((field_name, effective_output, label))
```

**Part 2**: extend `_types_compatible` to handle parameterized-generic producers. When a producer's type is `dict[str, X]` and the consumer's input is:
- `dict` (raw) → compatible (runtime `_is_instance_safe` will match)
- `dict[str, X]` → exact match (identity or `get_origin` + `get_args` comparison)
- `dict[str, Y]` where `Y != X` → incompatible, raise clear error
- `X` (the raw element type) → INCOMPATIBLE, raise with hint "upstream produces dict[str, X] via Each — consume the whole dict with input=dict or merge with a scripted node"
- `None` → skip (unchanged)

**Part 3**: update `_suggest_hint` to recognize "downstream wants X, upstream is dict[str, X] via Each" and suggest consuming as `dict` or `dict[str, X]`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Repro test at TDD red → green:
  ```python
  def test_each_downstream_raw_input_rejected(self):
      make = _producer("make", Clusters)
      verify = _consumer("verify", ClusterGroup, MatchResult).map(
          lambda s: s.make.groups, key="label"
      )
      summarize = _consumer("summarize", MatchResult, Summary)  # WRONG
      with pytest.raises(ConstructError, match="dict\\[str, MatchResult\\]"):
          Construct("bad", nodes=[make, verify, summarize])
  ```
- Positive test: downstream declares `input=dict` and passes:
  ```python
  def test_each_downstream_dict_input_accepted(self):
      ... same chain but summarize has input=dict ...
      Construct("good", nodes=[make, verify, summarize])  # no error
  ```
- Positive test: downstream declares `input=dict[str, MatchResult]` and passes
- Negative test: downstream declares `input=dict[str, OtherType]` and fails with element-mismatch message
- Hint test: error for raw input mentions "via Each" and suggests `input=dict` or `input=dict[str, MatchResult]`
- Existing `TestEach` and `TestNodeMap` end-to-end tests still pass (they don't chain past the Each'd node today, so they shouldn't regress)
- Same treatment for sub-constructs with Each modifier (state.py:75-81 applies)

## Dependencies
- **Blocks on neograph-ebe** — the fix in Part 2 requires the validator to recognize `input=dict` and `input=dict[str, X]` as valid deferred-to-runtime shapes, which is exactly what ebe fixes. Fix ebe first as a 3-line change, then this builds on it.

## Files a fresh agent needs
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:46-65` (producer accumulation — Part 1 fix)
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:180-210` (`_types_compatible` — Part 2 fix)
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:280-310` (`_suggest_hint` — Part 3 fix)
- `src/neograph/state.py:75-81, 119-124` (ground truth for modifier-adjusted state shape)
- `src/neograph/factory.py:287-336` (runtime `_extract_input` and `_is_instance_safe`)
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestConstructValidation` (test-class home)

## Not in scope (defer to later issues if needed)
- Oracle modifier effective-type tracking (Oracle keeps the raw output shape, so no bug)
- Sub-construct-with-modifier edge cases beyond what state.py:75-81 covers
- Introducing an explicit `EachResult[X]` wrapper type — a heavier option mentioned in the original report; the dict[str, X] tracking is lighter and matches what the state builder already emits.
close_reason: Wave 5: five parallel agents. edr: FromInput[T]/FromConfig[T]/default-value constants with param-resolution sidecar, 5 tests. 8k3: Each downstream type tracking fix (dict[str,X] producers, extended _types_compatible for generics, hint for raw/dict mismatch), 5 tests. 8vt: fan-in showcase example 01c (82 lines, diamond DAG, out-of-order declaration). uvb: Oracle dogfood (example 03 + TestOracle migrated). 4ou: Each dogfood (example 04 + TestEach migrated). 153/153 passing.

title: _merge_dicts duplicate key guard in factory (audit A4)
description: The state reducer _merge_dicts raises on duplicate keys, but factory _build_state_update doesn't check for duplicates when building keyed dicts for Each. If two fan-out items produce the same key, the error comes from deep in LangGraph's state management, not from neograph. Fix: add a guard in _build_state_update that raises a clear ConstructError when duplicate keys are detected.
close_reason: Added duplicate key guard in each_router (compiler.py) that detects duplicate dispatch keys before Send() fan-out. Two regression tests added in test_modifiers.py for @node and programmatic APIs.

title: Mini-project: Lead Research Pipeline
description:
## Structure
```
examples/lead-research/
  README.md              # narrative walkthrough
  pipeline.yaml          # neograph spec
  project.yaml           # types, tools, models
  main.py                # entry point: load_spec + compile + run
  tools/
    web_search.py        # Serper/Tavily API
    scrape.py            # website scraper (httpx + readability)
    enrich.py            # firmographic enrichment
  prompts/
    qualify.md           # ICP matching prompt
    score.md             # lead scoring prompt
    personalize.md       # outreach personalization prompt
  schemas.py             # Pydantic models (Lead, Company, Score, Outreach)
  .env.example           # API keys template
```

## Pipeline
1. fetch_leads (scripted) — read from CSV/CRM
2. research (Each over leads, sub-construct):
   - scrape_website (agent, tools=[scrape])
   - search_news (agent, tools=[web_search])
   - enrich (think) — synthesize findings
3. score (think, Oracle models=[reason, fast] for diversity)
4. qualify (think) — ICP match, branch: qualified/not
5. personalize (think, Loop: draft→review→revise until quality)
6. output (scripted) — write results to JSON/CSV

## Features demonstrated
- Each fan-out over leads
- Oracle multi-model scoring
- Loop for outreach refinement
- Sub-constructs for research phase
- Spec-driven (pipeline.yaml)
- Real API calls (Serper, httpx)
- Compile-time validation (show error when types mismatch)

## Narrative (README.md)
Walk through the pipeline step by step. Show the YAML spec. Show what happens when you change a prompt. Show the compile-time error when you wire types wrong. Show how to swap models.
close_reason: Built and verified: compiles, tests pass

title: @node: full fan-in parameter type validation (not just first param)
description:
Part of epic neograph-4mx (@node production readiness).

## Problem

`@node` fan-in works topologically but has partial static type validation. Only the FIRST parameter's type is checked against its upstream.

Repro:
    @node(mode='scripted', output=Report)
    def report(alpha: A, beta: B, gamma: C) -> Report: ...

    # Upstream produces B for alpha, A for beta (swapped), C for gamma
    @node(mode='scripted', output=A)
    def beta_producer() -> A: ...   # wrong type for report's `beta: B`

    @node(mode='scripted', output=B)
    def alpha() -> B: ...           # wrong type for report's `alpha: A`

    @node(mode='scripted', output=C)
    def gamma() -> C: ...

    pipeline = construct_from_module(mod)  # SILENTLY PASSES

The topology is wired correctly by parameter name (alpha→alpha, beta→beta, gamma→gamma), but the runtime raw_adapter will pass a B where `report` expects an A. No assembly-time error.

## Root cause

`src/neograph/decorators.py:138-143`:

    inferred_input = input
    if inferred_input is None:
        for p in sig.parameters.values():
            if p.annotation is not inspect.Parameter.empty:
                inferred_input = p.annotation
                break    # ← only first annotated param

The Node's `.input` field is a single type. `_validate_node_chain` only verifies that some producer satisfies `.input`. 2nd+ fan-in parameters are invisible to validation.

## Proposed fix

Add a dedicated validation pass in `construct_from_module` BEFORE handing nodes to `Construct(...)`. Does NOT edit `_construct_validation.py` (out of scope per epic policy — the current validator owns single-input checking).

Sketch:

    def _validate_fan_in_types(decorated: dict[str, Node]) -> None:
        for field_name, n in decorated.items():
            sidecar = _get_sidecar(n)
            if sidecar is None:
                continue
            fn, param_names = sidecar
            sig = inspect.signature(fn)
            for pname in param_names:
                param = sig.parameters[pname]
                if param.annotation is inspect.Parameter.empty:
                    continue
                upstream = decorated[pname]
                if upstream.output is None:
                    continue
                expected = param.annotation
                actual = upstream.output
                if not _types_compatible(actual, expected):
                    raise ConstructError(
                        f\"@node '{n.name}' parameter '{pname}' expects \"
                        f\"{_fmt_type(expected)} but upstream '{upstream.name}' \"
                        f\"produces {_fmt_type(actual)}\"
                    )

`_types_compatible` and `_fmt_type` live in `_construct_validation.py` — import them. Handles ForwardRef resolution via `typing.get_type_hints(fn)` (same pattern already used in `_resolve_field_annotation`).

Call `_validate_fan_in_types(decorated)` in `construct_from_module` right after cycle detection (after line 239), BEFORE the `_attach_scripted_raw_fn` loop.

## Acceptance criteria

- New test `test_node_decorator_fan_in_type_mismatch_raises` in `TestNodeDecorator`: constructs a fan-in @node where one param type doesn't match the upstream output, verifies `ConstructError` is raised from `construct_from_module` with a message naming both the expected and actual types, the offending parameter name, and both node names.
- New test `test_node_decorator_fan_in_all_params_validated`: 4-way fan-in where the LAST param has a mismatch, verifies it's caught (proves we don't just check 2nd param and stop).
- Existing `test_node_decorator_fan_in_three_upstreams` still passes — its types match correctly.
- Forward-ref resolution: test that `from __future__ import annotations` usage also triggers the check (run `typing.get_type_hints(fn)` against a module in that mode).
- Full suite stays green.

## Files to read

- `src/neograph/decorators.py:138-143, 191-214, 241-248` — current flow
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:_types_compatible`, `_fmt_type`, `_resolve_field_annotation` — helpers to import
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestNodeDecorator::test_node_decorator_fan_in_three_upstreams` — pattern to extend

## Non-goals

- Do NOT edit `_construct_validation.py`
- Do NOT change `Node.input` to be dict-valued
- Do NOT implement the scalar/run-input parameter support (separate issue in the epic)
close_reason: Three P1 @node production-readiness fixes landed in parallel team (node-p1-fixes). q54: mode inference (None default → scripted/produce inferred from kwargs) + decoration-time validation (LLM modes require prompt+model) + dead-body AST warning. 6 new tests. 8pt: full fan-in parameter type validation via _validate_fan_in_types in construct_from_module with ForwardRef resolution via get_type_hints+localns. 4 new tests. jyw: Each/fan-out interop via map_over=/map_key= kwargs on @node, sidecar carryover on modifier-copy, fan-out param skipping in adjacency loop, neo_each_item integration in raw_adapter. 6 new tests. Combined: 127/127 passing (+16 new). Examples 01b/02/08 cleaned up to use mode inference.

title: @node: replace or augment 01b example with a fan-in showcase
description:
Part of epic neograph-4mx (@node production readiness).

## Problem

`examples/01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py` is a linear 3-node port of `examples/01_scripted_pipeline.py`. Same shape, same output, just uses `@node` syntax instead of `Node.scripted(...)` + `Construct(nodes=[...])`.

This doesn't demonstrate why a user should reach for @node. A linear chain with @node vs. declarative is a lateral move — the user saves the `nodes=[...]` list but pays the price of `sys.modules[__name__]` + `construct_from_module(...)`. Zero net DX win.

The actual selling points of @node are:
1. **Fan-in without multi-field wiring** — `def report(claims: Claims, scored: Scored, verified: Verified)` just works
2. **Refactor-safety** — rename a function, downstream parameter reference breaks at import time
3. **Topology-from-signatures** — out-of-declaration-order dependencies sort themselves

None of these are visible in a linear 3-node chain.

## Proposed

Either replace `01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py` with a fan-in showcase, OR add `examples/01c_decorator_fan_in.py` as a new file. The showcase should:

1. Define 3-4 producer @nodes that emit different types (e.g., `fetch_claims`, `score_claims`, `verify_claims`, `gather_metadata`).
2. Define one consumer @node with fan-in: `def report(fetch_claims, score_claims, verify_claims, gather_metadata) -> Report`.
3. Define the nodes in a SHUFFLED order (not topological) so `construct_from_module`'s topo-sort is visibly doing work.
4. Include comment lines showing:
   - Which @node produces what
   - How the fan-in parameter names resolve to upstream nodes
   - Why this is nicer than `Node.scripted(input={'fetch_claims': Claims, ...})` (the existing dict-input pattern)
5. Run end-to-end with `compile` + `run` and print the aggregated report.
6. Stay under 100 lines — it's an example, not production code.

Use schemas that motivate the shape: e.g. requirements analysis (Claims, Scores, Verification, Metadata → Report) or code review (Files, Lint, Security, Coverage → Review).

## Acceptance criteria

- New example file at `examples/01c_decorator_fan_in.py` (keep 01b as the simple intro; add 01c as the fan-in follow-up).
- `python examples/01c_decorator_fan_in.py` runs without errors and prints a meaningful report.
- Example has a docstring at the top explaining what it demonstrates and contrasting it with 01 (declarative) and 01b (linear decorator).
- Out-of-order declaration proves topo-sort works (put the `report` node FIRST in the file).
- No new tests required — this is documentation-as-code. The existing `test_node_decorator_fan_in_three_upstreams` already covers the code path under test.
- Keep the example under 100 lines including docstring and blank lines.

## Files to read

- `examples/01_scripted_pipeline.py` — the original reference
- `examples/01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py` — the linear port to beat
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestNodeDecorator::test_node_decorator_fan_in_three_upstreams` — the pattern to pull into example shape

## Non-goals

- Do NOT delete or modify 01 or 01b.
- Do NOT use LLM modes in the example — stays scripted to avoid fake-LLM setup noise.
- Do NOT block on other epic subtasks — this example showcases already-working functionality.
close_reason: Wave 5: five parallel agents. edr: FromInput[T]/FromConfig[T]/default-value constants with param-resolution sidecar, 5 tests. 8k3: Each downstream type tracking fix (dict[str,X] producers, extended _types_compatible for generics, hint for raw/dict mismatch), 5 tests. 8vt: fan-in showcase example 01c (82 lines, diamond DAG, out-of-order declaration). uvb: Oracle dogfood (example 03 + TestOracle migrated). 4ou: Each dogfood (example 04 + TestEach migrated). 153/153 passing.

title: Compile-time: verify tool factory registration
description: At compile(), for every node with mode=agent/act and tools=[Tool('x'),...], verify each tool.name exists in _tool_factory_registry. Currently crashes deep in the ReAct loop at runtime. One loop over construct.nodes in compile().
notes: ## Research context: No other Python LLM framework validates tool registrations at compile time. LangGraph, PydanticAI, CrewAI, DSPy all defer to runtime. BAML validates but requires a DSL. This is low-hanging fruit to claim the strongest compile-time guarantee in the space.

title: Dogfood: migrate no-dep test classes (TestScriptedPipeline, TestExecuteMode, TestMiniRWPipeline, basic produce/gather) to @node
description:
Part of epic neograph-eiw (Dogfood @node). Tests that don't use Oracle / Each / Operator / sub-construct.

## Scope

Migrate the following test classes in `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py` from declarative `Node(...)/Construct(nodes=[...])` to @node:

1. `TestScriptedPipeline` — scripted linear pipeline
2. `TestExecuteMode` — execute mode with mutation tools
3. `TestMiniRWPipeline` — mixed scripted + produce modes
4. Basic parts of any other test class that constructs scripted/produce/gather pipelines without modifiers

## What to do

1. For each test, replace `Node.scripted("x", fn="fn_name", ...)` + `register_scripted("fn_name", lambda ...)` with:
   ```python
   @node(mode='scripted', output=...)
   def x(...) -> ...:
       return ...
   ```
2. Build the pipeline via `construct_from_module(mod)` where `mod` is a fresh `types.ModuleType(...)` with the decorated functions attached as attributes (same pattern as `TestNodeDecorator._fresh_module`).
3. Preserve the test's assertions exactly.
4. Run the migrated tests incrementally: `python -m pytest tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestScriptedPipeline -x` etc.

## Acceptance criteria

- All four target test classes use @node exclusively for pipeline construction.
- Every test's assertion set is preserved or strengthened (never weakened).
- Full test suite stays green.
- If a migration reveals an @node gap, STOP that test's migration and file a new subtask on neograph-4mx.
- Tests for modifiers (Oracle, Each, Operator, raw) remain on declarative — separate subtasks handle those.

## Files to read

- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestScriptedPipeline, TestExecuteMode, TestMiniRWPipeline` — current patterns
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestNodeDecorator::_fresh_module` — throwaway module helper
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestNodeDecorator::test_node_decorator_basic_chain` — end-to-end @node test pattern

## Non-goals

- Do NOT migrate `TestConstructValidation`, `TestConfigInjectionPatterns`, `TestErrorPaths` — they test the IR directly.
- Do NOT migrate Oracle / Each / Operator / raw / sub-construct test classes — separate subtasks.
- Do NOT delete `register_scripted` — it's still used by tests that keep declarative form.
close_reason: See prior close reasons. Force-closing because the subtask→epic dependency direction was set backwards at creation (subtask depends on epic instead of epic depends on subtask); cleanup will correct the direction across all epic subtasks in a single pass after this commit.

title: Oracle merge error message missing hint suffix

title: M4: Type auto-gen — required field not in properties silently ignored
description: spec_types.py: required list can name fields that don't exist in properties — silently ignored. The generated model is missing a 'required' field with no error. Fix: validate required ⊆ properties.keys().

title: MED-04: Remove silent try/except on Node field mutation
description: New (kqd refactor). decorators.py:964-967 and 972-975 wrap n.inputs and n.fan_out_param assignment in try/except (TypeError, ValueError): pass. Node is not frozen — these always succeed. Silent failure would leave wrong inputs or missing fan_out_param. Remove the guards.

title: Write regression test for neograph-uk10
description:
Write a failing test that defines the expected behavior for neograph-uk10.
This is the TDD "red" step -- the test MUST exist and MUST fail before
any implementation begins.

## Instructions

1. Identify what to test from the implementation plan
2. Choose test level (prefer integration over unit when DB involved)
3. Write the failing test
4. Use test factories for test data (never inline model construction)
5. Run and confirm it fails for the RIGHT reason (AssertionError, not ImportError)

## BANNED Patterns

- `pytest.mark.xfail` is BANNED in regression tests
- AST-scanning / source-code pattern tests are BANNED
- Direct DB session management in test code is BANNED

title: Implement neograph-uk10

then refactor.

1. **Green**: Write minimum code to make the failing test pass
2. **Verify**: Run the SAME test that FAILED -- must now PASS
3. **Refactor**: With passing tests as safety net

1. STOP. Do NOT modify the failing tests.
2. Re-read the Core Invariant.
3. If your approach violates the invariant -> revert and rethink.
4. If the test has a genuine bug -> document WHY before modifying.

Sacred rule: NEVER adjust tests to match code without justification.

```bash

```

title: Research neograph-kp12
description:

bead itself -- NOT the filesystem.

1. **Read the task**: `bd show neograph-kp12`
2. **Spec check** (if task touches schemas/protocol):
   - Read relevant specs and schemas
   - Check required vs optional, type constraints
3. **Explore the codebase**:
   - Search for relevant code
   - Read files that will need modification
   - Check existing tests for the affected area
   - Find 2-3 similar implementations to follow as patterns
4. **Check documentation** (doc-first rule)
5. **Engineering checklist** -- answer concretely:
   - DRY: Does similar logic exist?
   - Library idioms: How do your frameworks solve this?
   - Data flow trace: Walk one concrete example through the full chain
   - Existing conventions: What pattern do similar implementations follow?
   - Test infrastructure: What fixtures/helpers exist?
6. **Identify architecture decisions**
7. **Extract the core invariant** -- State in ONE sentence the architectural
   principle that ALL changes for this task must preserve.

## Store Research in the Bead

```bash
bd update neograph-kp12 --append-notes "## Findings
- [key findings]

## Relevant Code
- path/to/file.py:line -- [description]

## Risks & Edge Cases
- [potential issues]"

[One sentence: the architectural principle every change must preserve]

## Implementation Plan
1. [first step]
2. [second step]"
```

## Gate: Add Label

ONLY after notes AND design are written:

```bash
bd label add neograph-kp12 research:complete
```

title: Architect review of neograph-kp12
description:
Pragmatic architect review of the research findings for neograph-kp12.

## Precondition

Run `bd show neograph-kp12` and verify research:complete label is present.

## Review Criteria

1. **Invariant alignment**: Does EVERY step maintain the Core Invariant?
2. **Approach soundness**: Will the plan work?
3. **Risk coverage**: Are risks identified and covered?
4. **Pattern compliance**: Does the plan follow project architecture patterns?
5. **Specificity**: Is the plan specific enough to implement without guessing?
6. **Alternatives**: Was a better approach missed?

## Rate Each Finding

- **LOW**: Minor suggestion, cosmetic, nice-to-have
- **MEDIUM**: Real concern but agent can resolve autonomously
- **HIGH**: Fundamental issue -- user input required

## Write Review

```bash
bd update neograph-kp12 --append-notes "## Architect Review
- [RATING] Finding: [description]. Suggestion: [what to do]

Overall: [ALL_LOW | NEEDS_REFINEMENT | NEEDS_USER_INPUT]"
```
acceptance_criteria: Review written to task notes with rated findings and overall verdict.

title: Loop condition must be None-safe — undocumented requirement
description: compiler.py loop_router passes latest output to the condition lambda. On first iteration after the node runs, latest is the first output (not None). But if skip_when fires, latest could be from a previous iteration. Users must write 'd is None or d.score < 0.8' but this is undocumented. Fix: document in Loop docstring and modifier-kwargs.mdx.

title: Test suite migration: split monolith into 9 files + adopt BDD naming
description:
Split test_e2e_piarch_ready.py (86 classes, 6800 lines) into 9 focused files. Adopt property-based naming (test_{what_happens}_when_{condition}) during migration. Seven phases, each a single commit of class moves. No test logic changes except renames.

Target layout:
- test_pipeline_modes.py (~40 tests)
- test_modifiers.py (~80 tests)
- test_validation.py (~80 tests)
- test_node_decorator.py (~70 tests)
- test_forward.py (~50 tests, merge existing file)
- test_composition.py (~30 tests)
- test_renderers.py (~60 tests)
- conftest.py (shared schemas + fixtures)

Delete migration artifacts: TestNodeInputsFieldRename (5), TestNodeOutputsRename (7), TestExtractInputListUnwrap (3) — behavior covered by integration tests.

Naming convention: test_{what_should_happen}_when_{condition}. Class docstrings as feature specs.
close_reason: All 7 phases complete. 6997-line monolith → 7 focused files + schemas.py. 321 tests pass (15 migration artifacts deleted). BDD naming applied.

title: Migration P7: shared schemas → conftest.py + delete migration artifacts
description: Move shared Pydantic models (RawText, Claims, Clusters, MatchResult, etc.) and helper functions (_producer, _consumer) to conftest.py. Delete TestNodeInputsFieldRename (5 tests), TestNodeOutputsRename (7 tests), TestExtractInputListUnwrap (3 tests) — migration artifacts whose behavior is covered by integration tests.
close_reason: schemas.py created, 15 migration tests deleted, 321 tests pass across 7 files

title: Migration P3: extract test_validation.py
description: Move 8 classes (~47 tests) to test_validation.py: TestConstructValidation, TestConstructOracleErrors, TestFanInValidation, TestTypesCompatibleListOverDict, TestEffectiveProducerType, TestDictOutputsValidator, TestNodeDecoratorFanInValidation, TestNodeDecoratorFanInEachInterop. Rename methods. Add three-surface parametrize pattern for fan-in validation.
close_reason: 50 tests extracted to test_validation.py, BDD naming applied, 336 total pass

title: Migration P5: extract test_modifiers.py + test_composition.py
description:
test_modifiers.py (~80 tests): TestOracle, TestEach, TestNodeMap, TestOperator, TestOperatorContinues, TestOperatorResume, TestModifierAsFirstNode, TestConstructOracle, TestConstructEach, TestConstructOperator, TestDeepCompositions, TestNodeDecoratorFanout, TestNodeDecoratorOracle, TestNodeDecoratorOperator, TestListOverEachEndToEnd.

test_composition.py (~30 tests): TestSubgraph, TestMultiFieldInput, TestReducerEdgeCases, TestStateHygiene, TestStripInternalsEdge, TestDictOutputsStateModel, TestDictOutputsFactory, TestNodeInputsEpicAcceptance. Rename methods.
close_reason: 63 tests extracted: 34 to test_modifiers.py, 29 to test_composition.py, 336 total pass

title: Migration P6: remainder → test_pipeline_modes.py
description: Whatever remains becomes test_pipeline_modes.py: TestScriptedPipeline, TestProduceMode, TestGatherMode, TestExecuteMode, TestRawNode, TestMiniRWPipeline, TestOutputStrategy*, TestLLMConfig, TestConfigInjectionPatterns, TestRunIsolated, TestJsonModeOutputSchema, TestRenderPromptInspector, TestGatherToolCollection, TestLLMUnknownToolCall, TestErrorPaths, TestFirstNodeEdgeCases. Rename methods.
close_reason: Monolith renamed to test_pipeline_modes.py, 55 tests, 1405 lines. 336 total pass.

title: Migration P2: merge forward files → test_forward.py
description: Merge test_forward_construct_branching.py (8 classes) + ForwardConstruct classes from main file (TestForwardConstructBase, TestForwardConstructTracer, TestForwardConstructCompile) into test_forward.py (~50 tests). Rename methods. Delete test_forward_construct_branching.py.

title: Migration P4: extract test_node_decorator.py
description: Move 14 classes (~64 tests): TestNodeDecorator, TestNodeDecoratorModeInference, TestNodeDecoratorRawMode, TestNodeDecoratorParams, TestNodeDecoratorErrorLocation, TestNodeDecoratorCrossModule, TestNodeDecoratorDictInputs, TestDecoratorDictOutputs, TestFromInputPydanticModel, TestOracleMergeFnDI, TestToolDecorator, TestConstructFromFunctions, TestConditionalProduce, TestConstructLlmConfigDefault. Rename methods.
close_reason: 78 tests extracted to test_node_decorator.py, 336 total pass

title: Migration P1: extract test_renderers.py
description: Move 7 classes (~44 tests) to test_renderers.py: TestXmlRenderer, TestDelimitedRenderer, TestJsonRenderer, TestRenderInput, TestDescribeType, TestRendererDispatch, TestRendererThreeSurfaces. Rename test methods to test_{behavior}_when_{condition}. Cleanest cut — no deps on other test classes.

title: Establish baseline -- zero failures
description:

clean slate. Every failure after this point belongs to you.

## Instructions

1. **Run quality gate**:
   ```bash

   ```
   Record the exact pass count.

2. **Run integration tests** (if your project has them):
   Run the project's integration test command and record pass count.

## Gate -- HARD STOP on any failure

- Do NOT proceed to the next atom.
- Do NOT classify failures as "pre-existing" or "from another agent."
- Report the exact failures and leave this atom OPEN.

title: Oracle merge_fn — support @node-style dependency injection
description: Oracle's merge_fn is a plain function with signature (variants, config) -> output. It can't use FromInput/FromConfig parameter injection like @node does. Example pain: def _group_claims_merge(variants, config) needs to manually extract shared = SharedResources.from_runnable(config) and node_id = config.get('configurable',{}).get('node_id'). Should be: def group_claims_merge(variants: list[X], shared: FromConfig[SharedResources], node_id: FromInput[str]) -> Y. Either add a @merge_fn decorator, or let merge_fn reference a @node-decorated function that's called in merge context rather than as a topology node.
close_reason: 6jd: FromInput[PydanticModel] and FromConfig[PydanticModel] now detected at decoration time and resolved at runtime by pulling each of the model's field names from config['configurable'], constructing the instance. Classifier walks call-stack frames to find locally-defined Pydantic model classes (needed under 'from __future__ import annotations'). Shared _classify_di_params + _resolve_di_value helpers extracted so both @node and @merge_fn use the same DI machinery. 4 regression tests in TestFromInputPydanticModel. 9zj: new @merge_fn decorator for Oracle merge functions with FromInput/FromConfig DI. Auto-registers via register_scripted for back-compat with Oracle(merge_fn='name') lookups. make_oracle_merge_fn in factory.py checks the @merge_fn registry first and calls with resolved DI args instead of legacy (variants, config) signature when a match is found. Plain (variants, config) merge_fn functions still work unchanged. 3 tests in TestOracleMergeFnDI covering bundled FromConfig, FromInput, and back-compat. 217/217 passing.

title: node_start log fields inconsistent across modes
description: factory.py: scripted logs input_type+output_type, produce logs only output_type, gather/execute log tools+budgets but no types. All modes should log input_type and output_type as base fields. Source: code review MED-03, CON-07.

title: Condition expression evaluator for Loop/Operator when clauses
description:
## What
Spec-driven pipelines express conditions as strings: when: 'score < 0.8'. Need a safe evaluator that runs these against Pydantic model field values.

## Approach
- Parse simple expressions: field_name op literal (e.g. 'score < 0.8', 'confidence >= 0.9', 'passed == false')
- Supported ops: <, >, <=, >=, ==, !=
- Field access: dotted path (e.g. 'result.score < 0.8')
- NO arbitrary code execution — whitelist of ops, no function calls, no imports
- Return a callable(value) -> bool that the Loop/Operator modifier can use

## Deliverable
- parse_condition(expr: str) -> Callable in a new module (src/neograph/conditions.py)
- Tests: valid expressions, type coercion (string '0.8' → float), bad expressions raise clear errors

title: Sub-construct output boundary check doesn't fire for single-node sub-constructs
description: The output boundary check added in neograph-c4se doesn't catch the case where a single-node sub-construct declares output=TypeA but its only node produces TypeB. The check exists in _construct_validation.py line 192-212 but the producers list appears empty or the check doesn't fire for this specific case. Fixture at tests/check_fixtures/known_gaps/subconstruct_output_mismatch.py reproduces the issue.
close_reason: Already fixed by neograph-luzc (commit ff612b6). Boundary check now excludes input port.

title: E2E test: YAML spec → compile → run → correct output
description:
## What
End-to-end integration test proving the full pipeline: write a YAML spec file, load it, compile it, run it, assert correct output.

## Test cases
1. Simple pipeline: seed → refine (scripted, loop_when)
2. Multi-node loop: sub-construct with Loop modifier
3. Oracle ensemble: models=['reason', 'fast'] with merge
4. Variable substitution: ${node.field} in prompts
5. Project surface: types auto-generated from JSON Schema defs

## Approach
Use scripted mode (no real LLM) so tests are fast and deterministic. Register scripted functions that the spec references by name.

## Deliverable
- tests/test_spec_loader.py with 5+ e2e tests
- All pass with uv run pytest
close_reason: 16 honest E2E tests. Found and fixed multi-node sub-construct wiring bug. 528 passed.

description: Pre-existing. decorators.py — both functions have identical field_name collision check loops. Could push into _build_construct_from_decorated.
close_reason: Moved name collision check into _build_construct_from_decorated; callers now pass list[Node]

description: construct.py: name_: str = None — type says str but default is None. Should be name_: str | None = None. Source: code review MED-08, PP-09.
